attacking_style: Pure finisher who launches sprint from rival wheels in final 200m, prioritizing positioning conservation over early leadout commitment. Willingly skips intermediate sprints to maintain optimal finale position.
team_tactics: Quick-Step/Soudal relies on late-race disruption attacks (inside 10km) to fracture rival leadouts rather than sustained train control. Team deliberately repositions inside final kilometer after sitting back at 5km to avoid committing too early.
terrain_preference: Pan-flat bunch sprints with long straights (1-2km), especially technical street circuits. Completely eliminated by even moderate Cat-3 climbs in finales; echelon splits favoring positioning over power.
vulnerability: Cannot handle any climbing in finales—dropped immediately on moderate gradients. Struggles to sustain power through ultra-long straights (1.5km+) when forced to lead early. Vulnerable to mid-race form collapse requiring team to switch protected riders.
key_patterns: Won 6+ sprints from rival wheels without perfect leadout, excels when positioning beats power (2018-2021).
Eliminated by Cat-3 or harder climbs in finales; drops immediately when gradients appear (3 observations).
Team skips traditional leadout train, uses late attacks/disruption to fracture rival organizations instead (4+ instances).
Strategic intermediate sprint management: skips points to conserve position when green jersey math allows (2 observations).
Cannot sustain power through 1.5km+ straights when forced early; needs shorter finishing straights (2 observations).
2023 form collapse: dropped immediately on stage starts, team switched protected rider mid-race (2 negative signals).
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 Tour and Tirreno showed catastrophic form loss—dropped immediately at race start, team switched to backup sprinter. Sharp decline from 2021-2022 consistency.
